mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a structural break in labor demand that triggers corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not statistically negative when UNRATE breaches the 4.5% threshold across a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in consumer credit outstanding (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate synchronized demand and supply contraction, which compresses forward earnings expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of RSAFS and INDPRO declines does not predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, accelerating CPI readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity risk premiums and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving business and consumer confidence, leading to accelerated capital expenditure plans and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with median forward returns turning negative in the top quartile of housing growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 3.0%, it signals an overheating economy that historically precedes aggressive Federal Reserve tightening and equity valuation compression, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 3.0% does not correlate with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in a rolling 5-year window, or forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and the CPI index indicate wage-price spiral dynamics that erode real corporate margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL do not predict negative 20-day returns, with the interaction term showing a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained recovery phases, a divergence where industrial production declines while housing starts rise indicates inventory drawdowns and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence signal fails to generate negative 5-day returns when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark, or directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample periods.
